description_public: A polygenic estimate related to stress response tendency (hpa axis) variation in the general population.

CC.notes: This genotype is generally associated with a more typical FKBP5-related stress response. Life experiences, coping skills, and environment have a much larger influence than genetics alone.

CT.notes: This genotype has been linked to somewhat different regulation of the HPA axis in some studies, which may translate into a tendency toward stronger or more prolonged stress responses in certain situations.

TT.notes: This genotype has been associated with greater FKBP5 expression changes and altered feedback in the stress system. This does not predict any mental health condition but highlights the value of healthy coping strategies and support.

evidence.title: Placeholder evidence for Stress response tendency (HPA axis)
evidence.quote: This genotype is generally associated with a more typical FKBP5-related stress response. Life experiences, coping skills, and environment have a much larger influence than genetics alone.
